content:
0x01: The Stack This site is a static build generated by Hugo and deployed via GitLab CI/CD.
Generator: Hugo Editor: Zed Theme: PaperMod Depolyer: GitLab 0x02: Current Load I’m currently a CS student at UTSA currently taking these classes
Operating Systems Database Systems Data Science System Archietecture Principles of Cybersecurity 0x03: Active Projects Portfolio: This site, serving as a technical log. Bad X Clone: Social Media website for my Database Systems class. 0x04: Website Goal My overall goal with creating this website is to write down what i learn. I want something to look back on to remember, see how I’ve grown, and the steps I used to accompish a task. The plan for this is to write a blog about every week talking about something I accompished, including the steps on how I did so. I also hope this website gives me a practical purpose to create goals for myself; The largest challenge I’ve noticed is finding a reason to do something.
summary: Welcoming myself to my own website.

title: First Post

content:
Hi, I’m Christian 👋 I’m a Computer Science student at the University of Texas at San Antonio (graduating May 2027), focused on Software Engineering. I’m passionate about building efficient, secure systems and currently seeking a Summer 2026 internship.
Projects Penny Wise – Personal Finance Manager Lead Developer
A JavaFX desktop application that helps users visualize their finances by calculating income vs. expenses — replacing manual spreadsheet tracking.
Architected using the MVC pattern with a clean separation between a JavaFX frontend and Java backend Supports multiple user accounts stored locally via CSV files Designed end-to-end as a standalone local application Tech: Java, JavaFX, CSV, MVC
Bad X Clone – Social Media Platform Co-Developer
A collaborative LAMP stack social media website self-hosted on a Raspberry Pi, featuring a full relational database for user communication.
Built with MySQL, PHP, HTML, and CSS Configured and maintained on a Linux server running on a Raspberry Pi Designed and implemented a full relational database schema Collaborated with a development team to implement core social media features Tech: Linux, Apache, MySQL, PHP, HTML, CSS
Skills Languages: Java, Python, C, Bash, PHP, HTML, CSS, MySQL
Tools: Git, GitHub, VS Code, IntelliJ, Linux
Concepts: MVC Architecture, Database Design, Full Stack Development, OOP
Education B.S. in Computer Science
University of Texas at San Antonio | 2023 – Present (Graduating May 2027)
Relevant coursework: Data Science, Database Systems, Application Programming, Operating Systems
Contact 📧 christian26004@gmail.com 💼 LinkedIn 🦊 GitLab
title: Christian Burek
